title: erigeron chrysopsidis subsp. austiniae
intro: Erigeron chrysopsidis subsp. austiniae, also known as Austin's daisy or a subspecies of the fleabane plant, is a flowering plant native to North America. The plant is said to have medicinal properties, with its roots and leaves containing compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Erigeron chrysopsidis subsp. austiniae include its potential to alleviate digestive issues and reduce fever, as well as its use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. Erigeron chrysopsidis subsp. austiniae can be prepared for medicinal use through herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects and potential interactions.
